title: AI-Medical PGR Conference
text:
Submit Abstract   




The UKRI CDT in AI for Medical Diagnosis and Care is training over 50 graduates to become leaders in the application of artificial intelligence to healthcare. With a focus on research excellence, collaboration, and innovation, the Centre is committed to producing highly skilled professionals who can harness AI to develop secure, privacy-compliant solutions for medical diagnosis and care. By fostering industry partnerships and international collaborations, the Centre aims to position the UK as a global leader in health-related AI research and technology transfer.
Our annual conference brings together the CDT’s students, academics, and industry partners, offering a dynamic platform to explore and discuss the latest research from the CDT. Students will share updates on their progress, while our speakers will offer new perspectives to enrich the community. We hope that the event will provide an opportunity for our diverse network of researchers and healthcare professionals to reconnect and seek further opportunities for collaboration.















No matching items






We are pleased to invite Postgraduate Researchers (PGRs) at the University to submit abstracts for the AI-Medical PGR Conference 2026, a dedicated forum for showcasing innovative research at the intersection of artificial intelligence and healthcare. The conference is being organised by the UKRI Centre for Doctoral Training in AI for Medical Diagnosis and Care.



We encourage submissions including, but not limited to:

Clinical Decision Support: AI-powered diagnostics, predictive analytics, and personalized treatment planning.
Ethics & Governance: Algorithmic bias, data privacy, explainability (XAI), and regulatory frameworks.
Digital Health: Wearable technology, med-tech, digital twins, and AI-driven patient-centred care.
Drug Discovery: AI in pharmacology and large-scale cohort data analysis.





Format: No more than one side of A4 in the form of a structured abstract (Background, Methods, Results, and Conclusions). References and images may be included.
Word Limit: Maximum 300 words (excluding any references and image captions).




If your abstract is accepted, you will:

Present Your Work: Deliver a 2-minute spotlight presentation and showcase a research poster during a dedicated session.
Receive Expert Feedback: Engage in Q&A with leading clinicians, AI innovators, and senior academics.
Network & Collaborate: Join a multidisciplinary community of PGR students, industry and public sector partners to forge potential research collaborations.














    
        
        Ibrahim Habli
        University of York
